mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes exceeds 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no negative correlation with rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes exceed 0.3pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction that forces equity repricing downward over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with an accuracy above 50%, or the interaction term loses predictive power when added to a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side shocks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO declines during post-shock periods, or the signal reverses direction in subsequent quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the cost of capital becomes prohibitive for highly leveraged firms, triggering margin calls and forced selling that depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product growth signals strengthening economic activity that supports corporate earnings revisions, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no positive correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the predictive IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index growth above 3.0% annually forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% threshold, or the signal loses predictive accuracy after adjusting for nominal yield curve movements.
